findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add 'a benchmark harness script (e.g. `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/run_benchmark.sh`) that invokes the current gate over the mutant+control corpus and writes the per-class detection/FP table to a specified output file' to the Scope section so that AC3 has a corresponding step.
evidence: Scope names one script artifact: 'A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/.'
AC3 presupposes a separate 'benchmark harness' that runs the gate — this is a different artifact with different responsibilities
No step in the plan describes building or invoking a harness script
findings.finding: AC3 requires 'the benchmark harness' to run the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and record a baseline table, but the Scope section lists only 'a mutation-assembly script' — the harness is a distinct deliverable that has no corresponding step, meaning AC3 has no described step that reaches it.
findings.location: Scope section ('A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/.') vs AC3 ('The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class').
scenarios: An executor delivers the mutation-assembly script and labels, signs AC1/AC2/AC4, but AC3 cannot be satisfied because no harness was scoped or built.
The harness is assumed to be an existing tool, but if it doesn't exist yet the plan gives no instructions for creating it.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'A benchmark harness script at `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/run_benchmark.sh` that accepts the corpus directory as input, invokes the current gate on each plan, and writes `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/baseline_results.json` with per-class detection/FP counts.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command and output-artifact path to AC3, e.g.: `python run_benchmark.py --corpus docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ --output baseline_results.json && jq -e '[.classes | length == 5, (.classes[] | has("detection_rate") and has("fp_rate"))] | all' baseline_results.json`.
evidence: No grep/jq/diff command given to confirm the table exists and is structurally valid
'records' is vague about format (JSON? Markdown table? CSV?) and file location
No cardinality assertion that exactly 5 classes appear in the table with the required fields (detection rate, FP rate)
findings.finding: AC3's 'records the baseline detection/FP table per class' provides no proving command and no stated output artifact path, making it impossible to verify in-session whether the criterion has been satisfied.
findings.location: AC3: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class (the reference point every R-row's gate is judged against).'
scenarios: Executor marks AC3 done by pasting a table into a commit message rather than a queryable artifact, making it unverifiable by a subsequent reviewer.
Harness writes a partial table (missing one defect class) and no command exists to catch the omission.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define 'cleanly-closed' as a concrete, reproducible filter expression against the outcome corpus schema fields (e.g. `post_claim_edit_class == 'none' AND reopen_count == 0 AND force_close == False`) and add it to the plan or mutation-assembly script spec.
evidence: outcome_corpus.jsonl schema fields: ticket_id, ticket_type, level, post_claim_edit_class, reopen_count, force_close, completion_verifier_fail_count, review_round_count, had_persisted_review — no 'cleanly_closed' field
runs/README.md: 'Population: the 628 reviewed tickets (any ticket carrying >=1 REVIEW_RESULT plan-review sidecar)' — no clean-close filter
Class distribution: 'none 576, ac-strengthened 42, operator-attested-retag 7, substantive-unclassified 2, cosmetic 1' — many rows are explicitly NOT cleanly closed
mine_outcome_corpus.py build_corpus() includes all tickets where review_round_count > 0 with no clean-close filter
No definition of 'cleanly-closed' found anywhere in the harnesses, README, or corpus schema
findings.finding: The plan draws mutants from 'real cleanly-closed plans (drawn from the E1 outcome corpus)' but the outcome corpus (outcome_corpus.jsonl) contains ALL 628 reviewed tickets — including those with post-claim description edits (ac-strengthened, substantive-unclassified), force-closes, and reopened tickets. The term 'cleanly-closed' maps to no schema field, no documented filter expression, and no selection procedure anywhere in the corpus tooling or README. An executor has no codebase-grounded definition of which rows qualify.
findings.location: What section — 'Build a seeded-defect benchmark from real cleanly-closed plans (drawn from the E1 outcome corpus)'
scenarios: An executor pulls mutant source plans from tickets with post-claim ac-strengthened edits (42 of 628), inadvertently seeding controls that already have known defects — contaminating the FP bound
Two executors apply different informal interpretations of 'cleanly-closed' (e.g. post_claim_edit_class=='none' vs reopen_count==0 vs force_close==False), producing non-reproducible corpora
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What/Scope section: 'Source plans are drawn from outcome corpus rows where `post_claim_edit_class == "none" AND reopen_count == 0 AND force_close == False` — the zero-post-claim-edit population (576 of 628 rows per the corpus README).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an explicit seeded-mutant detection threshold for defect class 5 (untagged operational AC) to the R2 child AC or to the E2 plan itself, or explicitly state that class 5's pre-registered threshold is its own: 'N of 10 untagged-AC mutants detected by the DET lint, FP ≤ M of 10 controls'.
evidence: Epic ticket 6982 Context: 'GATE TERMS: each child R1-R7 story carries its own falsifiable pass/fail thresholds ... INSIDE its Acceptance Criteria - the child ACs are the authoritative gate rows; this epic does not duplicate the numbers'
R1 (59b7) AC: 'E2 detection >=80% on absent-capability mutants, blocking-FP <=10% on controls' — covers defect class 1
R3 (3a27) AC: '>=7/10 layer-cake/ordering mutants detected, <=1/10 controls flagged' — covers defect classes 2 and 3
R4 (03a9) AC: '>=8/10 E2 unnecessary-mechanism mutants with <=1/10 controls flagged' — covers defect class 4

R2 (b080) AC: '>=70% sampled precision (>=50-item sample) and <=5% overall flag rate' — covers defect class 5 but uses a LEXICON eval method, NOT a seeded-mutant detection threshold; no E2 detection rate specified for class 5
findings.finding: The plan instructs the executor to pre-register 'the per-class targets in the epic's gate table' for all 5 defect classes before any detector run, but no such single gate table exists: the epic ticket (6982) explicitly states it 'does not duplicate the numbers' — thresholds live in child ACs. More critically, defect class 5 (untagged operational AC) maps to R2 (b080), whose AC specifies lexicon precision/recall on the historical corpus — NOT a seeded-mutant detection threshold against E2 mutants. No per-class detection target for class 5 exists anywhere in the epic's gate structure.
findings.location: What section — 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table'
scenarios: Executor searches for a 'gate table' document that does not exist, cannot pre-register thresholds for class 5 (no seeded-mutant target exists), and either blocks on AC2 or arbitrarily assigns a threshold
The pre-registration commit omits class 5's detection threshold (because none is defined), making AC2's commit-order verifiability incomplete for 1 of 5 classes

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the benchmark harness (the script that invokes the current gate over mutants+controls and records the per-class detection/FP table) to the Scope section as a named artifact alongside the mutation-assembly script.
evidence: Scope says: 'A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/.'
AC3 says: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class (the reference point every R-row's gate is judged against).'
ABSENCE: No benchmark harness script is named or scoped in the Scope section.
findings.finding: The Scope section names only 'A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/' as the code artifact to create, but AC3 requires a distinct 'benchmark harness' that runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the detection/FP table per class. These are functionally different scripts (one assembles mutants; one invokes the live gate and collects results), yet only the mutation-assembly script is listed in Scope — the benchmark harness is an unscoped edit target.
findings.location: Scope section ('A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/') vs AC3 ('The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class')
scenarios: An implementer reads Scope, builds only the mutation-assembly script, and considers the ticket closeable — AC3 is silently unmet.
A reviewer checking scope completeness finds no scoped artifact corresponding to the AC3 harness requirement.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Scope line such as: 'A benchmark harness script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ that invokes the current gate (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/) over the committed mutant+control corpus and writes the per-class detection/FP baseline table.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the benchmark harness (gate runner) as an explicit Scope artifact and describe its mechanism in What: which gate entrypoint it calls, what inputs it takes (mutant corpus + labels), and what output schema it produces (the per-class detection/FP table).
evidence: Scope: 'A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/.' — only one script artifact listed.
AC3: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class (the reference point every R-row's gate is judged against).'

Cached prior review at .rebar/cache/plan-review/d125-b4ce-c57f-4f06/ independently surfaced this same gap: 'The Scope section lists only A mutation-assembly script as a code artifact, but AC3 requires a benchmark harness that runs the current gate over mutants+controls.'
findings.finding: AC3 requires 'the benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class', but the Scope section names only 'a mutation-assembly script' as a code artifact. No gate-execution harness is described in What or Scope. The mechanism for actually invoking the plan-review gate against the seeded corpus and recording results is entirely absent from the plan's specified deliverables.
findings.location: Scope section ('A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/') vs AC3
scenarios: An implementer following the Scope builds only the mutation-assembly script, then finds AC3 requires a gate runner with no specification of its interface, inputs, or output format.
The gate runner is built ad-hoc without a spec, producing results in a format incompatible with AC4's writeup requirement or the R-row gate rows that consume it.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the decision rule for AMBIGUOUS verdicts in the detection/FP counting protocol (e.g., 'AMBIGUOUS on a mutant counts as not-detected (FN); AMBIGUOUS on a control counts as not-fired (TN)').
evidence: harness.py line 62: verdict enum = ["PASS", "AMBIGUOUS", "FAIL"]
seedpilot.jsonl lines 4,6,7,8: GOOD plans (controls) receive AMBIGUOUS verdicts (e.g., G6/GOOD/repeat=0 -> AMBIGUOUS, T10/GOOD/repeat=1 -> AMBIGUOUS)
ABSENCE: plan specifies no rule for counting AMBIGUOUS in detection/FP tallies
findings.finding: The plan's benchmark harness must decide whether AMBIGUOUS gate verdicts count as 'detected' (TP on a mutant) or 'not detected' (FN). The gate emits PASS/AMBIGUOUS/FAIL (confirmed in harness.py), and seedpilot.py shows GOOD controls producing AMBIGUOUS at a measurable rate. This counting decision rule is referenced implicitly by 'records the baseline detection/FP table' but is never defined.
findings.location: AC#3: 'records the baseline detection/FP table per class'; What section: 'Pre-register detection thresholds'
scenarios: If AMBIGUOUS counts as 'detected', recall on mutants is inflated and FPR on controls is inflated — both distort the calibration.
If AMBIGUOUS counts as 'not detected', mutants that correctly triggered uncertainty are miscounted as misses.
Two harness implementers disagree on this rule and produce incomparable tables.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the README spec: 'For scoring purposes: on a mutant, FAIL = detected (TP), PASS or AMBIGUOUS = not-detected (FN). On a control, PASS or AMBIGUOUS = not-fired (TN), FAIL = false-positive (FP). Rationale: AMBIGUOUS is only correct when the plan genuinely under-specifies; the gate should FAIL on a mutant with a known defect.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the benchmark harness (the executable that invokes the current gate over mutants+controls and records the detection/FP table) as a named deliverable in the What section and Scope, with enough description to distinguish it from the mutation-assembly script.
evidence: Scope: 'A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/.' — no benchmark harness mentioned
AC3: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class'
What section describes committing mutants+controls+labels and pre-registering thresholds, but says nothing about a harness that executes the gate
findings.finding: The 'benchmark harness' that runs the current gate over mutants+controls (AC3) is not described or named in the What section or Scope — only a 'mutation-assembly script' is listed as a Scope deliverable. This leaves AC3 as an orphan criterion with no corresponding described work.
findings.location: Scope / AC3
scenarios: An implementer building only the mutation-assembly script (as Scope dictates) would not know to build or specify the benchmark harness, leaving AC3 unverifiable.
The harness may require non-trivial integration with the existing gate tooling — without a description it is under-specified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'A benchmark-runner script (e.g. docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/run_benchmark.sh or equivalent) that invokes the existing gate over each mutant and control, capturing per-class detection and FP counts into a machine-readable results file.' Mirror this in the What section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the benchmark harness (the script/tool that runs the current gate over mutants+controls and records the detection/FP table) as an explicit deliverable in the Scope section, alongside the mutation-assembly script.
evidence: Scope says: 'A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/'
AC3 says: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class'
These are distinct artifacts: one assembles the corpus, the other executes the gate and captures results — the harness is absent from the Scope deliverable list.
findings.finding: The Scope section lists only 'A mutation-assembly script' as a code artifact, but AC3 requires a 'benchmark harness' that runs the current gate over mutants+controls and records a detection/FP table — a distinct, non-trivial artifact not named in Scope. This mismatch means the deliverable list understates what must be built, risking the harness being treated as out-of-scope.
findings.location: Scope section (mutation-assembly script) vs. AC3 (benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls)
scenarios: An implementer reads Scope to understand what to build, sees only the mutation-assembly script, and omits the harness; AC3 then fails because nothing executes the gate and records results.
The harness is built but treated as an undocumented side-effort not subject to the scope's constraints (e.g., no gate/source changes).
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet list, add: 'A benchmark-harness script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ that runs the current gate over mutants+controls and writes a detection/FP results table (read-only use of the gate — no gate/source changes).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the output schema for the 'baseline detection/FP table per class' in the plan (field names, types, file path under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/).
evidence: Plan text: 'records the baseline detection/FP table per class'
Repo search for 'detection/FP table' returns zero hits
Existing harness outputs (seedpilot.jsonl, gate_run.jsonl) use ad-hoc schemas with no shared column standard
findings.finding: The plan requires recording a 'baseline detection/FP table per class' (AC#3) but never defines the output schema — no field names, file format, or column definitions are specified. The harness executor cannot build this output without a schema.
findings.location: AC#3: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class (the reference point every R-row's gate is judged against).'
scenarios: Two implementers build the harness independently and produce incompatible table formats — one uses JSONL, one uses CSV, neither matches what downstream R-row gate judgments expect.
The 'reference point every R-row's gate is judged against' cannot be used without a stable schema.
findings.suggested_fix: Specify e.g.: one JSONL row per (defect_class, mutant_id) with fields `{defect_class, mutant_id, label, verdict, detected: bool, fp: bool}` and a summary CSV with columns `{defect_class, n_mutants, recall, fpr}`.

evidence: 1 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).
vague/subjective terms present: clean
findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the label schema in the plan (e.g. 'each mutant is accompanied by a `<mutant_id>.label.json` file with fields `defect_class`, `control_file`, and `mutation_description`') so the mutation-assembly script and harness share a defined contract.
evidence: 'labels' format is unspecified — no schema, filename pattern, or example given
The harness in AC3 must read these labels to classify detections vs FPs; without a schema the harness cannot be built to spec
findings.finding: AC1 says 'committed with labels and the mutation diff' but does not define the label schema (e.g. a YAML front-matter field, a separate labels.json, a filename convention), leaving the executor to choose an arbitrary format that may be incompatible with the harness.
findings.location: AC1: 'each paired with its unmutated control, committed with labels and the mutation diff.'
scenarios: Executor commits labels as inline comments in the plan file; harness cannot parse them.
Two contributors add mutants using different label formats; the harness silently misclassifies some.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope or AC1: 'Labels are stored as `<mutant_id>.label.json` with required fields: `defect_class` (one of the 5 class names), `control_file` (relative path to the unmutated original), `mutation_description` (free text), and `mutation_diff` (unified diff string).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly add the baseline-results writeup (or expand the README's described content) in Scope to cover post-run detection/FP results and the mapping to gate rows, so AC4 has a corresponding described deliverable.
evidence: Scope: 'docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ (mutant corpus + labels + README documenting mutation method and pre-registered thresholds)' — no baseline-results writeup
AC4: 'A docs/experiments/ writeup records baseline results and how each gate row consumes them'
The README described in Scope covers mutation method and thresholds; AC4 requires post-run baseline results and gate-row mapping — content not covered by Scope
findings.finding: The 'docs/experiments/ writeup' that records baseline results and how each gate row consumes them (AC4) is not listed as a Scope deliverable. Scope only mentions a README under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ covering mutation method and pre-registered thresholds — a different document with different content.
findings.location: Scope / AC4
scenarios: Implementer follows Scope and produces only the mutation-method README; AC4 is then unverifiable because no baseline-results writeup was scoped.
If both documents are intended to be the same README, the content requirements in AC4 (baseline results, gate-row consumption) contradict the Scope description (mutation method, thresholds only).
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) add a separate deliverable 'A docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/baseline_results.md recording post-run detection/FP counts and gate-row consumption' to Scope, or (b) clarify that the README is a living document updated after the harness runs, and explicitly list both pre-run (thresholds) and post-run (results) content in Scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Embed the per-class detection thresholds (or a verbatim, stable path such as `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/gate-table.md`) directly in the plan so the executor can write the README pre-registration without consulting external context.
evidence: 'the per-class targets in the epic's gate table' — the specific numeric thresholds are not embedded in the plan or pointed to by a stable path
An executor writing the pre-registration README commit cannot proceed without knowing what values to record
findings.finding: The plan instructs the executor to pre-register 'the per-class targets in the epic's gate table' but neither embeds those target values nor provides a stable link/path to the gate table, so an executor cannot write the README pre-registration without locating external context.
findings.location: What section: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table.'
scenarios: Executor writes placeholder thresholds because the gate table is not findable from the plan text alone, invalidating the pre-registration guarantee.
Two executors independently produce different threshold values because each interprets the parent epic differently.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table inline in the plan (or reference `docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/gate-table.md#per-class-thresholds`) with the exact threshold values per defect class, e.g.: class 1 ≥80% detection, FP ≤10%, etc.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the harness script reads per-class detection thresholds from the pre-registered README/config (e.g., a structured YAML/JSON block in the README or a separate thresholds file) rather than hardcoding them, so the README remains the single source of truth.
evidence: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table'
'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class'
'Thresholds are pre-registered in the README' — plan is silent on whether the harness consumes these from the README or re-declares them
findings.finding: The plan does not specify whether the benchmark harness script reads per-class detection thresholds from the pre-registered README/config file or hardcodes them independently; if hardcoded in the script, threshold updates require dual-maintenance across the README and the script.
findings.location: Scope — mutation-assembly script; AC3 — benchmark harness; AC1/AC2 — pre-registered thresholds in README
scenarios: A reviewer adjusts a threshold in the README (the authoritative pre-registration location) but the harness script still uses the old hardcoded value, silently producing results against stale thresholds.
Threshold evolution across R-rows requires tracking two sources of truth.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a structured thresholds block (YAML front-matter or a separate `thresholds.yaml`) to the pre-registered README, and require the harness script to parse and consume it — making the README the sole authoritative source for both human readers and the script.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the mutation-assembly script's idempotency contract (e.g., fail-fast if output files already exist, or --overwrite flag with explicit confirmation) and document it in the README so re-runs never silently corrupt the labeled corpus.
evidence: AC1 requires >=10 mutants per class 'committed with labels and the mutation diff' but says nothing about what the script does on a second invocation
No mention of --overwrite, deduplication, or exit-on-conflict behavior
ABSENCE: no error-handling or partial-write recovery path described for the script
findings.finding: The mutation-assembly script has no specified idempotency behavior: if re-run after a partial failure or to correct a mutant, it is undefined whether it overwrites existing files, appends duplicates, or errors out — leaving the labeled corpus in a potentially inconsistent state with no error surfacing.
findings.location: Scope: 'A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/' and AC1
scenarios: Script crashes after writing 7 of 10 mutants for class 1; re-run silently appends 10 more, producing 17 entries with inconsistent labels
Script is re-run to fix a bad mutant; old and new versions coexist with the same filename stem, confusing the harness
findings.suggested_fix: Add a guard at the top of the script that checks for existing output files in the target run directory and either errors with a clear message ('corpus already exists; delete or pass --overwrite') or, with an explicit flag, replaces files atomically (write to a temp path, then rename).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the RETIRE/UPDATE lifecycle for run directories: specify a versioning or naming convention (e.g., runs/v1/, runs/v2/), document who owns a superseded run, and state whether old directories are archived or deleted when the corpus is regenerated.
evidence: Plan names CREATE (script) and CONSUME (harness) lifecycle nodes but says nothing about UPDATE ownership or RETIRE
ABSENCE: no versioning scheme, no policy for archiving or deleting superseded run directories
AC3 says the harness 'records the baseline detection/FP table per class' but does not say what happens to prior tables when the gate changes and the harness is re-run
findings.finding: The retirement/cleanup lifecycle for the mutant corpus is undefined: there is no specified path for what happens when a run directory is superseded (e.g., after a gate version change), so stale labeled corpora accumulate indefinitely under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ with no ownership or expiry policy.
findings.location: Scope: 'docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ (mutant corpus + labels + README)' and AC3–AC4
scenarios: Gate logic changes; harness is re-run producing a new baseline table alongside the old one; the writeup (AC4) references the wrong table
A mutant is found to be malformed; the corrected version is added but the old one is not removed, so the harness counts it twice

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or amend AC3/AC4) explicitly stating that the pre-registered thresholds — not the current gate's empirical baseline — are the pass/fail criterion for R-row gate acceptance, and that the baseline table is a diagnostic record only, not the acceptance bar.
evidence: 'records the baseline detection/FP table per class (the reference point every R-row's gate is judged against)'

'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector' — pre-registered thresholds exist, but AC3/AC4 make the CURRENT gate's empirical output the reference point for future R-rows, not the pre-registered thresholds
ABSENCE: no AC explicitly states that R-row gates must meet or exceed the PRE-REGISTERED thresholds (not merely the baseline table)
findings.finding: The baseline detection/FP table produced by running the CURRENT gate is designated 'the reference point every R-row's gate is judged against' — but if the current gate's empirical output (possibly well below pre-registered thresholds) becomes the comparison baseline rather than the pre-registered thresholds, the plan instantiates the change-detector/self-authored-oracle anti-pattern: it locks in current (possibly wrong) gate performance as the acceptance bar, defeating the purpose of pre-registration.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3 and AC4
scenarios: Current gate detects 4/10 mutants in class 3 but pre-registered threshold is 8/10; the baseline table records 4/10; future R-rows are judged as 'improvements' if they beat 4/10, never required to reach 8/10 — the benchmark closes green while the gate is still failing its own pre-registered bar
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC3 to: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class as a DIAGNOSTIC record. The pre-registered thresholds in the README are the acceptance bar; a gate row PASSES only when it meets or exceeds the pre-registered threshold for its class, not merely the baseline.' Add to AC4: 'The writeup explicitly compares baseline results to pre-registered thresholds and flags any class where the current gate is below threshold.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific file/section that contains the 'epic's gate table' (the source of per-class thresholds) so the README commit in AC2 can be verified against it.
evidence: What section says: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table.'
AC2 says: 'Thresholds are pre-registered in the README in a commit that precedes any detector run against the corpus (verifiable by commit order).'
ABSENCE: No specific file path for the 'epic's gate table' is given; a search for 'gate table' and 'per-class targets' across the repo returns no matches.
findings.finding: AC2 references 'the per-class targets in the epic's gate table' as the source for pre-registered thresholds, but no specific file path or section is cited for this gate table. The plan does not name which existing document holds the gate table, leaving implementers to guess where the thresholds originate.
findings.location: What section ('Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table') and AC2
scenarios: Implementer cannot locate the gate table and either invents thresholds or delays work to find the right source document.
Pre-registered thresholds diverge from what the epic actually specifies because the source was ambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the authoritative source of per-class detection thresholds (name the epic and file path, or explicitly state the implementer must set them a priori), and confirm that source already exists before any detector run — or specify that E2 itself establishes the initial threshold values, removing the circular dependency.
evidence: Plan text: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table.'
No epic is named; no gate table file path is given; search of docs/ finds no pre-existing per-class seeded-defect threshold table.
The plan's own Why section states E2 gates R1 and R3, suggesting E2 is upstream of the gate table, not downstream of it.
findings.finding: The plan says to pre-register thresholds drawn from 'the per-class targets in the epic's gate table', but neither the identity of that epic nor the location of its gate table is specified anywhere in the plan. If the gate table is itself derived from experiments like E2, there is a circular dependency: the thresholds cannot be drawn from a table that E2 is meant to help establish. This hidden ordering assumption means AC2's commit-order guarantee ('a commit that precedes any detector run') could be vacuous — the pre-registration step has no authoritative source to copy from.
findings.location: What section / AC2: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table'
scenarios: At implementation time, the implementer opens the README to fill in pre-registered thresholds and finds no gate table to copy from, so they invent values — breaking the independence guarantee that pre-registration provides.
The epic's gate table exists but contains thresholds calibrated for a different criterion set than the 5 defect classes E2 targets, leading to mismatched per-class targets.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] For each of the 5 defect classes, specify which gate criterion id(s) are expected to fire on that class's mutants, so the benchmark harness and the AC3 detection table have an unambiguous definition of 'detected'.
evidence: Plan lists 5 classes by mutation description only, with no criterion id or gate-row reference.
AC3 requires a 'per class' table but the gate has ~37 criteria; without a class→criterion mapping, 'detected' is undefined.
The existing seedpilot.py maps each seeded defect explicitly to a criterion id (e.g. G6→TOCTOU, A1→plugin-registry); this plan omits the equivalent mapping.
findings.finding: The 5 defect classes are defined by their symptom/mutation type but are never mapped to the specific gate criteria (e.g. criterion ids like E1, R3, G6, A1) they are intended to exercise. Without this mapping, AC3's 'baseline detection/FP table per class' is ambiguous: a harness implementing the spec wouldn't know which criterion's pass/fail verdict determines whether class (3) ('consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge') or class (5) ('untagged operational AC') is detected.
findings.location: What section: defect class list (1)–(5) / AC3: 'baseline detection/FP table per class'
scenarios: The harness runs all 37 criteria against every mutant and calls a class 'detected' if any criterion fires — diluting per-class signal and making the FP table meaningless.
Different implementers map class (4) 'unnecessary mechanism' to different criteria (A1 vs G6 vs a custom criterion), producing incomparable results across runs.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table to What or the README: | Defect class | Primary criterion | e.g. (1) absent asserted capability → E1; (2) layer-cake split → R3/decomposition; (3) artifact-without-ordering-edge → dependency criterion; (4) unnecessary mechanism → A1; (5) untagged operational AC → attestation criterion.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Re-parent E2 to a ticket whose declared scope explicitly covers seeded-defect benchmark construction (e.g., the plan-review-gate experiment epic), or add E2's benchmark deliverables to the parent's (d39b) plan and re-review the parent — the current parent's scope is confined to a single SOTA report and explicitly excludes implementation work.
evidence: Parent Non-goals: 'NO code changes to the gate in this task — recommendations only; implementation is follow-up work to be filed as discovered_from tickets.'
Parent Scope/Files: 'New file: docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md (the committed deliverable; recommendations included as a section). Read-only analysis of: src/rebar/llm/plan_review/ ...'

E2 Scope: 'docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/ (mutant corpus + labels + README documenting mutation method and pre-registered thresholds). A mutation-assembly script under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/. NO gate/source changes.'
E2 AC3: 'The benchmark harness runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls and records the baseline detection/FP table per class' — no harness or gate-run appears in parent's plan.
Parent R3 falsifiable experiment describes a seeded-flaw eval conceptually as a future validation step, not as a parent deliverable.
findings.finding: E2's entire scope (seeded-defect benchmark corpus, mutation script, benchmark harness, and writeup) falls outside the parent's (d39b-8c45-385c-418a) declared deliverable scope, which is confined to a single research report (docs/research/task-decomposition-sota-2026.md). The parent's Non-goals explicitly exclude implementation: 'NO code changes to the gate in this task — recommendations only; implementation is follow-up work to be filed as discovered_from tickets.' E2 is not delivering part of what the parent's plan contains; it delivers something the parent explicitly placed out of scope.
findings.location: E2 Scope section; parent d39b-8c45-385c-418a Non-goals / Scope / Files
scenarios: An implementer reads the parent's plan to understand what E2 is delivering part of — they find no overlap; the parent's contract does not authorize the benchmark corpus, mutation script, or gate-run harness.
A reviewer validating E2's completion against the parent's ACs finds none of E2's four ACs map to any parent AC, since the parent's ACs are all about the SOTA report content.
findings.suggested_fix: If E2 is intended as follow-on implementation of R3/R7 experiments from the SOTA report, its parent should be an experiment-series epic (such as the plan-review-gate experiment container) rather than the closed SOTA-report task. Alternatively, if d39b is meant to be the authorizing parent, add a deliverable section to d39b: 'Experiment infrastructure: seeded-defect benchmark as specified in R3's falsifiable experiment, under docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/' — then re-review d39b against its own rubric.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'gate table' schema — column names, value vocabulary, and file location — that pre-registered per-class detection thresholds will be written into, co-located in the plan or README spec.
evidence: Plan text: 'the per-class targets in the epic's gate table'
Repo search for 'gate table' returns zero hits across all docs/
Repo search for 'per-class target' returns zero hits
ABSENCE: no file in docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ or its README defines this table
findings.finding: The plan references 'the per-class targets in the epic's gate table' for pre-registered thresholds, but this 'gate table' schema — its location, format, and value vocabulary — is never defined anywhere in the plan or found in the repo.
findings.location: What section: 'Pre-register detection thresholds BEFORE running any detector: the per-class targets in the epic's gate table.'
scenarios: The README author goes to write pre-registered thresholds and has no defined table structure or value range to fill in, so they invent ad-hoc numbers with no shared vocabulary.
The harness author cannot know what format to compare against or what fields to populate.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a table definition to the README spec, e.g.: `| defect_class | min_recall | max_fpr | notes |` with one row per class and concrete numeric targets (e.g., min_recall ≥ 0.70, max_fpr ≤ 0.10 per the Tricorder cliff cited in the Why section).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit mapping from each of the 5 defect classes to the gate criterion ID(s) whose FAIL verdict constitutes a detection for that class.
evidence: Plan names 5 defect classes by informal description only
criteria_v8.json (confirmed present) uses criterion IDs: E1, F1, G1G2, G3, G4, A1, T8, etc.
ABSENCE: no mapping from defect class label to criterion ID appears anywhere in the plan
The gate runs all applicable criteria — without a mapping, the harness cannot determine which criterion verdict signals detection for each mutant class
findings.finding: The plan names 5 defect classes with informal labels (e.g., 'absent asserted capability', 'layer-cake split') but never maps them to the gate's actual criterion IDs (E1, F1, G3, etc.). The AC#3 harness 'runs the CURRENT gate over mutants+controls' and must know which criterion to check for a detection per class.
findings.location: What section, defect class enumeration: '(1) absent asserted capability... (2) layer-cake split... (3) consumed-artifact-without-ordering-edge... (4) unnecessary mechanism... (5) untagged operational AC'
scenarios: 'Absent asserted capability' maps to E1 in the existing corpus (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/README.md references E1-verified miss class), but the plan never says so.
The harness author scans all criterion verdicts for any FAIL, inflating apparent recall, or guesses the wrong criterion ID and computes an incorrect detection rate.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
